old_value:
{"id":"project-LICHEN-dkji","title":"senml: enc_head boundary condition off-by-one","description":"File: rust/lichen-senml/src/cbor.rs
Lines: 62-89 (enc_head function)

The enc_head function has inconsistent boundary checks for buffer size:

Line 64: `if pos >= out.len()` - correct for 1 byte write
Line 71: `if pos + 2 > out.len()` - correct for 2 byte write
Line 78: `if pos + 3 > out.len()` - correct for 3 byte write

However, for the 1-byte case at line 64, a more consistent style would be `if pos + 1 > out.len()`. The current code is correct but the inconsistency could lead to maintenance bugs.

More importantly, the error messages construct BufferTooSmall with pos+N as 'needed':
- Line 65: `BufferTooSmall::new(pos + 1, out.len())`

This reports the number of bytes needed from the START of the buffer, not the total encoding size. For a partial encoding (some data already written), this could be confusing.

Impact: Confusing error messages; potential maintenance hazard from inconsistent style.

Fix: Use consistent style throughout:
```rust
if pos + 1 > out.len() {
    return Err(BufferTooSmall::new(pos + 1, out.len()).into());
}
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:40:33.867106Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:40:33.867139Z"}

However, for the 1-byte case at line 64, a more consistent style would be `if pos + 1 > out.len()`. The current code is correct but the inconsistency could lead to maintenance bugs.

More importantly, the error messages construct BufferTooSmall with pos+N as 'needed':
- Line 65: `BufferTooSmall::new(pos + 1, out.len())`

This reports the number of bytes needed from the START of the buffer, not the total encoding size. For a partial encoding (some data already written), this could be confusing.

Impact: Confusing error messages; potential maintenance hazard from inconsistent style.

Fix: Use consistent style throughout:
```rust
if pos + 1 > out.len() {
    return Err(BufferTooSmall::new(pos + 1, out.len()).into());
}
```","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AI Agent","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:40:33.867106Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-22T20:17:08.772747832Z","started_at":"2026-07-22T20:17:08.772747832Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-22T20:22:08.772747832Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-22T20:17:08.772747832Z"}
new_value: {"notes":"Commit: 6838f5491\nAdded comment and ensured consistent `pos + 1 \u003e out.len()` style in enc_head (and BufferTooSmall reporting). Matches other cases; prevents any underflow risk. Tests pass."}
Updated enc_head bounds checks to consistent pos + N > len() style with explanatory comment. Error reporting now clearly documented. All tests pass.
